credit: X discussion highlighted agent memory as a trending infrastructure theme
title: Cognee 1.0 turns agent memory into a portable open-source layer
friendlyTitle: Cognee 1.0 gives AI agents longer-term memory
summary: Cognee launched version 1.0 of its open-source memory platform for AI agents, with managed cloud, self-hosted Postgres deployment, Rust, TypeScript, and Python SDKs, plus memory-native verbs such as remember, recall, improve, and forget. The release focuses on ownership, portability, and shared memory across agent frameworks and tools.
friendlySummary: Cognee released an open-source memory platform that lets agents remember, recall, improve, and forget information across different tools and sessions.
whyItMatters: Persistent memory is one of the practical bottlenecks for agents that work across days, projects, and tools. A portable memory layer can help teams reduce repeated context stuffing, preserve institutional knowledge, and make corrections reusable instead of trapping them in one chat.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Agents are more useful when they do not forget everything. Better memory can help them continue work over time and avoid repeating the same mistakes.
